article_title: Google Splits Its Eighth-Generation TPU Into Training and Inference Chips as Anthropic Locks in Up to a Million Units

content_preview.title: Google Splits Its Eighth-Generation TPU Into Training and Inference Chips as Anthropic Locks in Up to a Million Units
content_preview.summary: At Cloud Next 2026, Google unveiled the TPU 8t and TPU 8i, its first-ever split TPU line, with Anthropic as anchor customer for up to one million chips across 2026 and 3.5 gigawatts in 2027.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Google used its Cloud Next 2026 keynote on April 22 to unveil its eighth-generation Tensor Processing Unit line, breaking with a decade of unified design by splitting the TPU into two purpose-built chips: the TPU 8t for training and the TPU 8i for inference. [Google's official announcement](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/infrastructure-and-cloud/google-cloud/eighth-generation-tpu-agentic-era/), authored by SVP and Chief Technologist Amin Vahdat, frames the move as a response ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece at 804 words (within the 400-1200 range). Clear Overview / What We Know / Broader Cloud Next 2026 Announcements / What We Don't Know sections. Technical depth is appropriate and accurate. Appropriate internal link to the March 19 Meta-TPU leasing article provides continuity.

source_verification.blog.google_eighth-generation-tpu-agenti: VERIFIED. Confirms author Amin Vahdat (SVP and Chief Technologist, AI and Infrastructure), training/inference split, 9,600 chips per superpod, 121 ExaFlops, 2 petabytes HBM, near-linear scaling to 1 million chips, double ICI vs Ironwood, 2x perf/watt vs Ironwood, 97% goodput, 288 GB HBM and 384 MB SRAM (3x prior gen) for 8i, 19.2 Tb/s ICI, Collectives Acceleration Engine reducing latency up to 5x, 80% better perf/dollar, and 'agentic era' framing. Article is dated April 22, 2026.
source_verification.cloud.google.com_ai-infrastructure-at-ne: VERIFIED. Confirms all TPU 8i specs cited: 384 MB SRAM (tripled), 288 GB HBM, 19.2 Tb/s ICI bandwidth (doubled), Collectives Acceleration Engine, 5x latency reduction for high-concurrency, 80% better perf/dollar, KV Caches hosted entirely on silicon. Also confirms A5X with Nvidia Vera Rubin NVL72 and TPU 8t 9,600 chips / 121 exaflops. April 22, 2026.
source_verification.cnbc.com_google-launches-training-and-in: PARTIALLY VERIFIED. CNBC returns 403 to automated fetches (bot block common for CNBC) and could not be directly retrieved. However, the three substantive claims attributed to CNBC are independently corroborated: (a) the 'nearly 3x' / ~2.8x price-performance vs Ironwood language matches Google's own blog ('nearly 3x the compute performance per pod'); (b) the Nvidia-complementary positioning is confirmed by the Google Cloud infrastructure post and TechCrunch; (c) A5X with Vera Rubin later in 2026 is confirmed by the Google Cloud post. Article URL uses CNBC's standard dated path for April 22, 2026.
source_verification.techcrunch.com_google-cloud-next-new-tpu: VERIFIED. Confirms million+ TPU clustering claim and Nvidia-complementary positioning with Vera Rubin. Author Julie Bort, April 22, 2026. Note: the '16 billion tokens per minute' figure is attributed in the article body to TechCrunch, but the verified source for that specific figure is the Google/Pichai keynote blog, which is also cited alongside. Not a misattribution concern since both sources are present and Google's blog is the primary.
source_verification.thenextweb.com_google-ironwood-tpu-infer: VERIFIED. Confirms TPU 8t as Broadcom-designed codenamed Sunfish; TPU 8i as MediaTek-designed codenamed Zebrafish; both targeting TSMC 2nm for late 2027; 44.6% annual custom ASIC growth vs 16.1% GPU growth; 45% of AI accelerator market by 2028; Anthropic access to up to one million TPU chips and >1 GW in 2026, expanding to 3.5 GW in 2027 across Ironwood and new 8-series. Nvidia inference share claim in the source is framed as 'could fall from more than 90%' which is the same >90% figure cited in the article. Author Cristian Dina, April 22, 2026.
source_verification.9to5google.com_google-workspace-intellig: VERIFIED. Confirms Workspace Intelligence as unifying AI layer across Gmail, Docs, Chat; 'understands complex semantic relationships' language; priority action surfacing; AI Inbox in Gmail; 'Ask Gemini' in Google Chat. Author Abner Li, April 22, 2026.
source_verification.blog.google_cloud-next-2026-sundar-picha: VERIFIED. Confirms Pichai's 40% QoQ growth in Gemini Enterprise paid MAU in Q1 2026; 'over 16 billion tokens per minute' via direct API; introduction of Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform; expanded Wiz partnership on AI Application Protection Platform. April 22, 2026.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All verifiable claims map accurately to the cited sources. Specs (9,600 chips, 121 exaflops, 384 MB SRAM, 288 GB HBM, 19.2 Tb/s, 5x MoE latency, 80% perf/dollar, 97% goodput, 2x perf/watt) all trace to Google's own technical blog and Google Cloud infrastructure post. Anthropic commitment figures (up to 1M chips / >1 GW in 2026, 3.5 GW in 2027 across generations) trace to The Next Web. Cloud Next secondary announcements (Gemini Enterprise growth, 16B tokens/min, Agent Platform, Wiz AI-APP) trace to the Pichai keynote blog. Workspace Intelligence details trace to 9to5Google. No hallucinated quotes detected — the direct quoted phrases ('goodput', 'agentic era', 'understands complex semantic relationships', 'over 16 billion tokens per minute', 'Ask Gemini') all appear verbatim in their respective sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The 'What We Don't Know' section explicitly notes unpublished pricing, ambiguous GA timing vs the 2027 volume-production date, and the open question of whether custom ASICs can erode Nvidia's inference dominance. No sensationalism; no AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Original. No prior Machine Herald coverage of Cloud Next 2026 or the TPU 8t/8i announcement. The article appropriately links to the March 19 Meta-leasing-Google-TPUs piece as context rather than duplicating it. PR #1008 covers narrower cloud-infrastructure angle on the same news and will be handled separately in this batch.
concerns: CNBC source (cnbc.com/2026/04/22/google-launches-training-and-inference-tpus-in-latest-shot-at-nvidia) returned 403 to automated fetches. This is CNBC's standard anti-bot behavior, not a dead link. The CNBC-attributed claims are independently corroborated by Google's own blog and the Google Cloud infrastructure post, so there is no unsupported claim; noting for provenance transparency.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, well-sourced News piece on a major industry announcement. All seven sources verified (six directly, one — CNBC — via independent corroboration of its attributed claims due to 403 bot-block). Technical specs match Google's primary sources verbatim; Anthropic commitment figures match The Next Web; Cloud Next secondary announcements match the Pichai blog and 9to5Google. Approved for publication.
